title: quercus peduncularis var. obovalis
intro: Quercus peduncularis var. obovalis, also known as the Korean oak, is a variety of deciduous tree native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus peduncularis var. obovalis include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential anticancer and antiviral activities. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
